title: Zettelkasten Linkage

content.statement: Connect notes to build knowledge web.
content.question: Do your notes connect, or are they a graveyard of isolated thoughts?
sop: Pick one note-taking tool that supports linking, and commit to it
Every time you add a note, link it to 2-3 existing related notes
Make links bidirectional so you can navigate the web from either direction
Rate your notes' connectedness by sampling 10 at random and counting their links (see calculation, page 6)
Revisit orphaned notes (zero links) monthly and either connect or archive them
content.explainer: A note sitting alone can only tell you what it already says, it can't surprise you, because it has no relationship to anything else you know. The value of a Zettelkasten isn't the individual notes, it's the links between them, because that's where a new idea you didn't deliberately write down can actually emerge.
questions: Do your notes connect, or are they a graveyard of isolated thoughts?
When did you last go back and link an old note to something you just learned?
If you opened your notes right now, could you trace a chain of three connected ideas, or would every note be an island?

definitions.def: A note-taking method where each note is deliberately linked to related notes, so knowledge forms a connected web instead of a pile of isolated entries.

content.equation_example: Connectedness: rate 1-10 how many links the average note has (10 = richly connected). At a 2, notes sit isolated and are hard to rediscover. At an 8, one note leads to five related ones, and patterns surface on their own. Higher connectedness means your notes actively generate insight; lower means they're just a storage bin you rarely revisit.
content.closing_statement: Linked notes teach you things isolated ones can't.

caption:
Principle Nº 224: Zettelkasten Linkage

Connect notes to build knowledge web. A note sitting alone can only tell you what it already says, it can't surprise you, because it has no relationship to anything else you know.

Ask yourself: Do your notes connect, or are they a graveyard of isolated thoughts?
